message:
feat(abi): implement native named semaphores via /dev/shm + mmap, replacing glibc callthrough

Replace the glibc-callthrough implementations of sem_open, sem_close, and
sem_unlink with fully native versions backed by /dev/shm/sem.NAME files and
mmap'd shared memory regions.

Key implementation details:

- resolve_sem_path: validates POSIX semaphore name rules (must start with '/',
  no further slashes, length <= 251 chars after prefix) and constructs the
  canonical /dev/shm/sem.<name> path
- sem_open: opens/creates the backing file via SYS_openat, truncates to
  SEM_MMAP_SIZE (32 bytes, matching glibc sem_t), mmap's MAP_SHARED, and
  atomically initializes the futex word with the caller's initial value on
  creation; closes fd after mapping (mapping keeps file open)
- sem_close: unmaps the shared region; backing file remains for other processes
- sem_unlink: removes the /dev/shm backing file via SYS_unlinkat; existing
  mappings remain valid until all processes close

This eliminates three GlibcCallThrough symbols (sem_open, sem_close,
sem_unlink) in favor of direct syscall + mmap implementations, advancing the
project toward full libc independence. The futex word in the mapped region
is compatible with the existing unnamed semaphore (sem_wait/sem_post/
sem_trywait/sem_timedwait) implementations that use futex-based atomic
operations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
